subject: Congratulations, Mike Braun
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Indiana Republican Party
body:
It's been a busy primary season. Republicans across the state have been working long hours -- making phone calls, knocking on doors, and working... | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/indiana/congratulations-mike-braun?e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| | [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=1dd2e8b1f6&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| Friends --

On behalf of the Indiana Republican Party, I want to congratulate our Republican nominee and next U.S. Senator -- Mike Braun!

It's been a busy primary season. Hoosiers across the state have been working long hours -- making phone calls, knocking on doors, and working the polls in support of our Republicans candidates throughout the state. And no matter which one of our great Republican U.S. Senate candidates you supported during this primary \-- Mike Braun, Luke Messer and Todd Rokita -- it's now time to come together.

That's because Mike Braun will provide President Trump with a new partner in the Senate, working to advance conservative, Hoosier values. He's got a track record of delivering results, and he'll provide the firewall to prevent obstructionist Democrats from causing even more gridlock in Washington.

Mike Braun will also take this race directly to Democrat Joe Donnelly. He'll remind Hoosiers that Donnelly isn't the moderate he claims to be. Donnelly is a liberal Democrat, who has a history of voting in lock-step with his Democrat bosses, Nancy Pelosi and Chuck Schumer. But it doesn't stop there. Donnelly is a lazy legislator who was ranked as the the least effective Democrat in in the U.S. Senate -- and that's really saying something.

Tonight, I'm excited to offer my support to Mike Braun, and I invite you to join me.

Respectfully,

Kyle Hupfer
Chairman, Indiana Republican Party

subject: President Trump ends U.S. participation in the flawed Iran deal
body:

## The Day Ahead
| • | President Donald J. Trump will host a meeting of his Cabinet to discuss the threats emerging from America’s broken immigration system. [Learn more.](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=029b7ea0c4d3c59b9c2c71f2d0681fa88ca276000684de1f44584df5913f701e08d9027cc7367654003e03c48f2eae727294c0fd5c08588e)
---|---
• | The President will sign an Executive Order this afternoon on Military mothers and spouses. [Watch live at 3:10 p.m. ET. ](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=f1397017c828f759a247d7be9dcaba8a1a4171b2bc46c26eddddb77b9f3757e865e8cca1136a8e0915c40b1ab5729a1a3189c5fb222b29b0)

---
|

## President Trump ends U.S. participation in the flawed Iran deal
**“The Iran deal is defective at its core,”** President Trump said yesterday in a speech from the White House Diplomatic Reception Room. “If we do nothing, we know exactly what will happen. In just a short period of time, the world’s leading state sponsor of terror will be on the cusp of acquiring the world’s most dangerous weapons.”
**The President announced that America will withdraw from the deal.** The agreement has failed to protect America’s national security interests, enriched the Iranian regime, and allowed Iran to preserve nuclear research and development. New evidence released by Israel also confirms that Iran’s leaders made the deal in bad faith, lying about past efforts to develop nuclear weapons.
**The U.S. will immediately begin reimposing sanctions.** These penalties will target crucial sectors of Iran’s economy and pressure the regime to alter its course of malign activities across the Middle East. They will also ensure that global funds stop flowing toward terrorism.
[Learn more about President Trump’s decision to withdraw from the Iran nuclear deal.](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=f1397017c828f75931415d1361b1716e647346d7186b6a8015782fc4c8a804755b19d6cb2a64cdb59c0f99cd493ddceb0b981f984ba9a3ac)
**Watch** : [Key points from yesterday’s speech](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=f1397017c828f7598ca99e78c60a281890c53462710a26781b3115e22178a26e3ae2503790eadc80ec6531178e1444453317285abaec8fca)

---
|

## Gina Haspel has defended our national security
**Today, the Senate begins its process to confirm Gina Haspel** as Director of the Central Intelligence Agency with a hearing before the Senate Intelligence Committee. While Haspel is expected to earn confirmation, some Senators are putting political interests above national security by opposing her nomination.
**Haspel is an award-winning career intelligence officer** who has drawn praise from both Republican and Democratic former national security leaders. By threatening to vote against her, some Senators are attempting to block the first woman ever nominated to be Director of the CIA, a 33-year veteran of the Agency, and the first career CIA officer to be nominated as Director in 52 years.
**Despite repeatedly facing false or misleading allegations against her** , the facts show that Gina Haspel has served her Nation honorably. Current and former intelligence officials attest that she is “unfailingly honest,” “professional,” and has “high moral character.”
[See the facts about Gina Haspel—and why attacks on CIA public servants won’t work. ](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=f1397017c828f7594b77da03ff9ebf700dd2a1e51e58c78d30b25b7f77248c068d5f1b0c750fc2f16a872fd42fcdf6862dc5d5e87057e7ef)

---
|

name: Tom Perez
subject: I need two minutes to say why, Derek:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, www
body:
This is an investment in our party -- and also an investment in our country. It's an investment in the future we're leaving for our children and grandchildren.
---
Derek --

Before I ask you to make a monthly sustaining contribution to the Democratic Party, I'd like to take a minute to tell you why I'm asking now.

At this moment, Democratic candidates all across the country are building out their campaigns to take on Republicans in every state. It's not going to be easy. But we have a real shot to take back Congress this year -- and the stakes are way too high for any one of us to sit this one out.

The months to come are a critical time to invest in Democrats, and I am counting on you to sustain our fight for the long haul. Contributing monthly is one of the most effective ways to support Democrats running in competitive races nationwide this year while building our party for the long term. So I am asking you today:

**Make a $3 monthly sustaining contribution right now to help Democratic candidates take on the GOP this election cycle and beyond.**

**[ DONATE: $3](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/405228cd/4067b0a/58d65d50e/7e440be1/2793282212/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/may-sustainer-2018?recurring=true&amount=3&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=3+-+DONATE+3&utm_campaign=em_20180509_tp_nd&source=em_20180509_tp_nd&refcode=em_20180509_tp_nd")**
Contributing monthly is a big commitment -- but it's also an investment. It's an investment in our party and our country. It's an investment in the future we're leaving for our children and grandchildren. By starting a monthly donation in May, you will have an incredible impact on our party's ability to win nationwide in November and beyond.

You don't need me to tell you that this is a critical year for Democrats. Together we have a chance to not only take back Congress and flip state legislatures -- we can build our party's infrastructure to ensure Democrats keep winning in the years to come. No one else is going to get the job done, Derek.

When Democrats celebrate victories up and down the ballot in November, you can know that your monthly contribution was what made those victories possible.

**[Contribute $3 today to start a monthly donation to the Democratic Party.](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/405228cd/4067b0a/58d65d50e/7e440bec/2793282212/VEsA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/may-sustainer-2018?recurring=true&amount=3&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=6+-+Contribute+3+today+to+start+a+monthly+do&utm_campaign=em_20180509_tp_nd&source=em_20180509_tp_nd&refcode=em_20180509_tp_nd")**

All my best,

Tom

Tom Perez
Chair
Democratic National Committee

Contributions or gifts to the Democratic National Committee are not tax deductible. Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, www.Democrats.org, and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If this isn't your preferred email address, [update your contact information](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/405228cd/4067b0a/58d65d50e/7e440bed/2793282212/VEsB/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/user/manage?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=7+-+update+your+contact+information&utm_campaign=em_20180509_tp_nd&source=em_20180509_tp_nd&refcode=em_20180509_tp_nd"). Our email list is the best way we have of staying in regular contact with supporters like you across the country and letting you know about the work Democrats are doing. If you like staying in touch, but want to receive only the most important messages, click [here](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/405228cd/4067b0a/58d65d50e/7e440bea/2793282212/VEsO/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/signup/less-email?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=8+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20180509_tp_nd&source=em_20180509_tp_nd&refcode=em_20180509_tp_nd"). Click [here](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/405228cd/4067b0a/58d65d50e/7e440beb/2793282212/VEsP/ "http://www.democrats.org/unsubscribe?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=9+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20180509_tp_nd&source=em_20180509_tp_nd&refcode=em_20180509_tp_nd") to unsubscribe from our supporter list, but if you leave, it will be harder for you to stay involved in the organization that you've been such a critical part of. This organization is powered by you, and we'd love to hear your ideas. Send us any comments, criticisms, or feedback [here](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/405228cd/4067b0a/58d65d50e/7e440be8/2793282212/VEsHBQ/ "http://my.democrats.org/page/s/contact-the-democrats?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=10+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20180509_tp_nd&source=em_20180509_tp_nd&refcode=em_20180509_tp_nd"), or just reply to this email! Thanks for supporting our party.

name: Andrew Gillum
subject: Do you want to be a superhero?
body:
[](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb30aa/9e00690/44cd3482/2402104a/463353219/VEsH/ "https://andrewgillum.com/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=gillum&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=em18_180509_WaVote_fnd_gq_nono_1_AG&source=em18_180509_WaVote_fnd_gq_nono_1_AG")
Friends --

When _Black Panther_ hit theaters, it became one of the highest grossing movies in America -- and it's clear to see why it's become such a cultural phenomenon.

Not only was it historic in having an African-American director and predominantly Black cast, it tackled themes of race and identity head-on, rather than sidestepping it. All while representing black men and women in positive ways the silver screen has rarely shown.

And in turn, _Black Panther_ inspired #WakandaTheVote, a political movement dedicated to empowering Black voters.

In Florida, we have the chance to make history this November -- but everyone needs to make their voice heard in order to make that happen. **[So we're joining the #WakandaTheVote effort -- and with it, a new bumper sticker to help spread the word. Chip in whatever you can afford to get yours right now!](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb30aa/9e00690/44cd3482/2402104d/463353219/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gillum-wakanda-the-vote?gpsid=13_55_344&refcode=em18_180509_WaVote_fnd_gq_nono_1_AG&utm_campaign=em18_180509_WaVote_fnd_gq_nono_1_AG&utm_medium=email&utm_source=agil&utm_content=2+-+So+wersquore+joining+the+WakandaTheVote+")**
[](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb30aa/9e00690/44cd3482/2402104c/463353219/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gillum-wakanda-the-vote?gpsid=13_55_344&refcode=em18_180509_WaVote_fnd_gq_nono_1_AG&utm_campaign=em18_180509_WaVote_fnd_gq_nono_1_AG&utm_medium=email&utm_source=agill&utm_content=3")
---
Just like Wakanda, we need to defend our communities and collectively work to improve it from within. We can all be superheroes, and we can all be activists for change. Together, we're proving that when we mobilize, we can truly change the outcome of elections, change the government, and change our future for the better.

**[So please, chip in whatever you can afford to get your sticker and help #WakandaTheVote this year.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb30aa/9e00690/44cd3482/2402104f/463353219/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gillum-wakanda-the-vote?gpsid=13_55_344&refcode=em18_180509_WaVote_fnd_gq_nono_1_AG&utm_campaign=em18_180509_WaVote_fnd_gq_nono_1_AG&utm_medium=email&utm_source=agil&utm_content=4+-+So+please+chip+in+whatever+you+can+affor")**

Bring it home,

Andrew

**[GET THE STICKER](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb30aa/9e00690/44cd3482/2402104e/463353219/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gillum-wakanda-the-vote?gpsid=13_55_344&refcode=em18_180509_WaVote_fnd_gq_nono_1_AG&utm_campaign=em18_180509_WaVote_fnd_gq_nono_1_AG&utm_medium=email&utm_source=agil&utm_content=5+-+GET+THE+STICKER")**

body:
Hi Peter,

Two weeks ago, more than 300 friends, neighbors, and community members celebrated the opening of our new campaign headquarters. Our office is not just an office, but a welcoming home for our campaign volunteers and supporters, and our new space is a true reflection of that warmth. | **Saint Albans Messenger
Thursday May 3, 2018** **Editorial: The Bigger Issue** **_By Emerson Lynn_** As the Vermont Legislature steams toward adjournment the public’s attention is being focused on the spat between the Governor and legislators as to how a $58 budget hole in the education fund is to be filled. That “hole” represents roughly three percent of what we spend on our schools each year. It is an issue, but not a show-stopper. It’s more political drama than anything of serious substance. What is serious, and what is not being addressed, is the state of our preK-16 educational system, and its relationship to the broader Vermont economy. It’s a story of numbers and it is a story that will define our social and economic future. In the mid-1990s we were educating 104,000 students in our preK-12 schools. Today, we educate 75,000. That number is expected to drop to 69,000 in the next five years, and drop to 65,000 by 2030. That loss of 40,000 students plays out in a variety of ways. Not only does it require us to adapt our present educational structure to a much lower number, it means our room for error has also been reduced in equal measure. Employers are already starved for able-bodied people willing to work. As the student count declines, the workforce numbers decline. This is problematic in the recruitment of businesses to locate here, and it’s problematic to keep those who are here and who have plans for growth. That means the few who are available to work must have the skills required. That gets us back to the educational system - specifically higher ed. As has been duly reported, Vermont has one of the highest high school graduation rates, but one of the lowest percentage of graduates opting for a four-year college degree. As our student population declines, so, too, does the number that considers furthering their education. This is particularly troublesome for the Vermont State College System, made up of Northern Vermont University [Lyndon and Johnson State], Castleton University, Vermont Technical College and Community College of Vermont. The overwhelming percentage of those attending any of these schools are from Vermont, and many of them are the first in their families to attend college. With the exception of CCV, the colleges struggle in the competition to attract enough students to fill their rosters. The colleges are also among the most expensive to attend, largely because most states subsidize their schools to a far greater degree than Vermont does. [Of the 50 states, we rank among the lowest.] Lyndon State, Johnson State and Castleton are particularly vulnerable. If the “experiment” with the merger of Lyndon State and Johnson State into Northern Vermont University fails, it’s hard to see how both campuses remain open. Not only would that be a blow to the community or communities involved, it would reduce the educational opportunities for Vermont students, and, by extension, it would weaken the employment pool for Vermont employers. So, what’s the plan? What are we doing to address this potential? It’s not being addressed, at least not in a blue-ribbon panel fashion, one staffed by the state’s best thinkers, and those who have the most at stake. That is why the appeal of the governor’s five-year educational plan - banking savings of almost $300 million - is so compelling, but in ways the governor is not articulating. We can’t confine the definition of our school system as something from preK through the 12th grade. It needs to include the early education component - zero to five years old. And it needs to include higher education. This gets back to limiting our margin of error. The challenge in education is, and has always been, the bottom 40 percent of our school population. Those who elect not to go to college, or to further their education. The ones who probably knew that about themselves before the sixth grade. This happens for a variety of reasons, but two loom above all others: the first is low expectations and a lack of opportunity at an early age, the second is expense. College tuition is above what this cohort can afford; expectations are ratcheted down from an early age. If we are operating a school system with 40,000 fewer students, then that 40 percent becomes crucial. We can’t afford to let any of them slip through the system; not when tomorrow’s work force demands higher and higher skill sets. This need will not be accomplished within the preK-12 system that exists. It needs to be addressed as a complete system, recognizing the role of early education [including child care issues] and higher ed. This raises the obvious turf issues. The preK-12 system is where the money is and those who have it can, and do make a case as to why they need every nickel they have. If there is a silver lining to the decline in our student population it’s that it’s forced us to reassess these assumptions, to admit our new reality. That is the opening the governor’s plan provides.

I'm deeply concerned about what's going on in the White House right now: Ending Robert Mueller's investigation into Russian interference in our elections would cripple our democracy.
But President Trump is refusing to take this investigation seriously. This investigation isn't a "witch hunt," it's a matter of national security and critical to protecting our democracy.** Every step the president takes to undermine this investigation puts our nation at risk of further attacks. **
The American people need to know the extent of Russian interference in our elections - and we need to protect Robert Mueller so he can get to the bottom of this. That's why I'm supporting a bill in Congress to protect the special counsel from political meddling - but Mitch McConnell and Republican leadership won't bring it to the floor.
**I won't give up the fight until the American people have answers once and for all. But the future of our democracy is at stake, so I need you to add your voice to mine:**
**[Add your name to tell Congress: Protect Robert Mueller!](https://petitions.signforgood.com/ProtectRobertMueller?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&code=shaheen&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Fcjs_protectmueller%3Frefcode%3Djtk848-ja-nd)**
Thank you,

Jeanne

name: Gov. Mike Huckabee
subject: We must not miss this warning sign
body:
Peter,
I can’t believe what I’m seeing right now. I don’t even know how to describe it other than comparing it to a terrible storm that’s brewing on the horizon.
This time, it’s not coming from the Washington Swamp – it’s actually on the opposite side of the country, in California.
You may have heard the news or seen the reports, but people of faith in California are bracing for one of the most dramatic ideological shifts away from the first amendment in our nation’s history. It’s the kind of storm that destroys the foundations of liberty and a free society.
AB 2943, a bill overwhelmingly passed by the California Assembly (50-18) and soon to be voted on in the State Senate, would make illegal any sale of goods or services that seek to change an individual’s sexual orientation, behaviors or gender expressions.
It prevents faith-led counselors from assisting clients with same-sex attraction or gender confusion. It prohibits churches from holding any ticketed events addressing topics of sexuality.
**But it’s FAR more reaching than that.**
This means the sale of ANY book that states the practice of homosexuality or transgender identification as immoral actions would be illegal in California. **Peter, this could include the Bible!**
Sponsors of the bill argue that it will not prohibit the sale of the Bible or other books. But the language of the bill suggests otherwise. Legal experts agree that AB 2943 is so broadly worded in its description of prohibited activities that it can easily be interpreted to stop the sale goods and services that promote a biblical worldview!
This is a massive attack on Christianity. It should alarm us that the Constitutional problems inherent in the bill were not obvious to those in the California Assembly. Passage of the bill would mark a significant and rapid decline of religious freedom and freedom of speech as we know it in California.
Christian conferences have already been canceled in California and moved to other states!
The bill has not even been signed into law yet and Christians are preparing for the worst.
I’m reminded of these words from Dietrich Bonhoeffer, “First they came for the Communists, but I was not a Communist so I did not speak out. Then they came for the Socialists and the Trade Unionists, but I was neither, so I did not speak out. Then they came for the Jews, but I was not a Jew so I did not speak out. And when they came for me, there was no one left to speak out for me.”
The ideology that Christians should be silenced is threatening Californians.
And if we think we can simply pull out of California, leaving the state to face the consequences of this bill and thinking it won’t affect the rest of the country, we are foolishly ignoring the words of Bonhoeffer and the lesson history has taught us.
What’s happening in California is our warning. It’s our wake-up call to stand, and it couldn’t be coming at a more important time.
All 80 State Assembly Seats in California are up for election this year – 50 of these seats held by members who already voted to approve AB 2943!
But it’s not just California that is in the midst of an ideological storm, and it’s not just California that has the opportunity to do something about it.
**In 2018 over 8,500 elections are taking place all over the country.**
**People of faith MUST mobilize.** If we don’t stand united for our Judeo-Christian values at the polls then we can expect the ideology that’s taking root in California to grow and spread to the rest of the nation.
I’m asking for your help. The election season is already here.
We need to reach 90-million Christians in America to vote for candidates who uphold Biblical principles.
[We are actively engaged in helping our brothers and sisters in California and we need your help too! Please make your best donation today. If we hope to win against an invasive ideology that threatens religious liberty we must win big in California and in states and communities across the nation.](https://www3.myfaithvotes.org/p180509apl01?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180509apl01nd&n=1)
Together, we can send a message to the country – when 90-million Christians stand united - we are a powerful force.
Thank you for heeding this warning from California and making a contribution to see Christians show up to the polls across the nation.
[](https://www3.myfaithvotes.org/p180509apl01?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180509apl01nd&n=2)
**United. We Stand.**

Gov. Mike Huckabee
Honorary National Chairman
[](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2F&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180509apl01nd&n=3)
©2018 My Faith Votes
10940 S Parker, 640, Parker, CO 80134
This email was sent to [dpwillis67@gmail.com](dpwillis67@gmail.com).
To stop receiving emails, [click here](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/unsubscribe?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180509apl01nd&n=4).

Tomorrow, Donald Trump is holding a campaign rally in Elkhart. [This is not a coincidence.](https://act.myngp.com/el/smvjnAnK-V5pENyvsnutNbqApWKAL_ycXc66ZNgHw8k=/6XhLtYPAQm9wBMUrbM42t5-4hmwAJP58neY9iNeOGnE=?refcode=180509-email) We're in the General Election now. We are fighting against the continued partisanship and out-of-touch policies of Jackie Walorski, and we can expect that special interest groups from across the country will be stepping up to protect her. Jackie Walorski has been hiding behind failed policies, corporate interests, and party-line votes for too long -- and it's clear she is terrified of this campaign. Frankly, she should be. Her constituents deserve better, and now we are demanding it. Thirty years ago, when I drove into Detroit to serve as a minister, I thought to myself that maybe this farm boy from Indiana had gotten in a little over his head. But we didn't try to change the entire city all at once. We didn't even try to change an entire neighborhood. Rather, we looked at what we could realistically accomplish and got to work. Block by block. House by house. Neighbor by neighbor. **That 's the type of community-building that this campaign is going to do, and we will not rest -- not even for one day. We have six months to work as hard as we can.** Let me be clear: this campaign is my job -- and I have an incredibly smart and driven team working around the clock to run a campaign that can beat Jackie in November -- but we need you, too. We need for you to speak up. Trust in institutions is at an all-time low -- and people don't trust what they read on the Internet. This campaign needs people like you to talk to everyone you know. Because while voters may not trust Facebook ads or TV commercials, they trust _you._You_ _are our most important messenger. **[We can't look back in November and wish we had done more. Can you pitch in $25 today and be one of the first donors to our General Election campaign?](https://act.myngp.com/el/smvjnAnK-V5pENyvsnutNbqApWKAL_ycXc66ZNgHw8k=/6XhLtYPAQm9wBMUrbM42t5-4hmwAJP58neY9iNeOGnE=?refcode=180509-email)** If you can't donate today, that's okay. Be our messenger. [Share this on Facebook with your friends and family, and let them know who _you_ are voting for in November.](https://act.myngp.com/el/smvjnAnK-V5pENyvsnutNbqApWKAL_ycXc66ZNgHw8k=/EmEUjTA9dSzNnBksjUP6as9HGZnO1GJmn-6ybZ8ZdIE=?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.facebook.com%2Fmelforcongress%2Fposts%2F1790545707678153) _Remember: Block by block. Neighbor by neighbor._ Politicians in Washington like Jackie Walorski spend way too much time talking at each other, around each other, or about each other instead of doing what needs to get done for the American people. They are creating deep, painful divides within our communities. We will do the opposite -- and it will start with this campaign. **We need to fight now, so that we can fight for a better healthcare system.** Every American has the basic right to see a doctor when they're sick. Jackie Walorski has voted over 50 times to kick people off healthcare. People should not live in fear that healthcare could be taken away. **We need to fight now, so that we can fight to fund our schools.** If we prioritize it, we can provide our children the resources they need to succeed, so that all kids have an equal chance to achieve their own American Dream. **We need to fight now so that we can fight for an economy that works.** Everyone who works an honest day's work deserves an honest wage. **We need to fight now so that we can help veterans, and provide them the care they deserve.** When our heroes return home, our leaders too often keep their hands in their pockets and their mouths shut. We do not provide adequate mental healthcare for our veterans, and these heroes are forced to wait too long to receive their care. We must do better. **We need to fight now so that we can protect Social Security and Medicare.** Hard-working men and women should be able to retire with dignity and spend time with their loved ones. Jackie Walorski wants to privatize Social Security. I will protect it. It is NOT a bargaining chip. **We need to fight now so that we can rebuild our infrastructure.** We can create jobs without saddling states and municipalities with the bill. [Please chip in here.](https://act.myngp.com/el/smvjnAnK-V5pENyvsnutNbqApWKAL_ycXc66ZNgHw8k=/6XhLtYPAQm9wBMUrbM42t5-4hmwAJP58neY9iNeOGnE=?refcode=180509-email) Thanks for all that you've done and all that you will continue to do. Let's GO! Mel | Paid for by Mel Hall for Congress

I've gotten to know some of these candidates over the last few months and let me tell you: we've got a good group.

Over the coming weeks, we'll introduce you to many of those candidates, but for now let's let them know that we have their back.

name: Tim Ryan
subject: Thank you, friend
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tim Ryan for Congress
body:
I'm still a little tired from staying up to watch the election results roll in, but all I can say is this: Thank you. | |
---
Friend, I'm still a little tired from staying up to watch the election results roll in, but all I can say is this: **Thank you.** I am honored to have won my primary election, and humbled to have the support of Democratic voters in the 13th Congressional District. Representing the community I grew up in and care so deeply about in Washington is the honor of a lifetime. **With the general election ahead of us, I make this promise to you:** If re-elected, I will continue to fight for Northeast Ohio in the Halls of Congress and use my position on the House Appropriations Committee to ensure that federal investments and resources come back home. I will keep working to enact legislation that will make college more affordable, revitalize America's cities, and improve the health and well-being of American families and children. Every person in this wonderful country has value and deserves to be given the tools necessary to live a fulfilling life -- regardless of their gender, ethnicity, faith, or sexual orientation. That is my fight. That is my mission. Thank you. **On to November! ** \-- Tim [Contribute ->](http://action.timryanforcongress.com/page/m/d7a9563/5486deaa/28e54505/dcc2a95/15042200/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tr_may18?refcode=em20180509_NEohio_ty_30")

Tim Ryan is a champion for working families in Ohio's 13th District and is known for challenging both parties to do more to rebuild the middle class. Tim was elected to the U.S. House of Representatives in 2002 and has been fighting for working people ever since.

subject: Keeping Tina Smith in the Senate this November

body:
Tina's gotten to work fighting for legislation that will make a difference. | [](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc48c19/c1f7015/688c6c68/11434402/2429452504/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/murray-for-smith?utm_source=ema_2018-05-10_murray&refcode=ema_2018-05-10_murray&utm_campaign=murray&utm_medium=ema") Dear Friend, Tina Smith has spent her entire life working hard to solve problems, and she's willing to listen and work with anyone in order to get the job done. She's even been nicknamed the "Velvet Hammer" for her ability to build positive relationships while never giving in to bullies or sacrificing her values. **That's exactly the kind of person we need in the Senate.** After serving as Minnesota's lieutenant governor for three years, traveling around the country and hearing Minnesotans' hopes and needs, she joined us in the Senate last January and has shown she's a fierce advocate for an economy that works for everyone, quality public education, our farmers, our veterans, and our environment. [**But this November, Tina faces a special election to keep her new role as U.S. Senator, and it's up to us to ensure we do everything we can to keep her in the Senate. Will you chip in $5 or more today to show Tina you have her back?**](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc48c19/c1f7015/688c6c68/11434402/2429452504/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/murray-for-smith?utm_source=ema_2018-05-10_murray&refcode=ema_2018-05-10_murray&utm_campaign=murray&utm_medium=ema") Already, Tina's gotten to work fighting for legislation that will make a difference:

* And she's joined me in fighting to make sure **all families have access to high-quality, affordable child care.**

name: Brittni McGuire
subject: Let's go!
disclaimer_text: Paid for and authorized by the WV State Democratic Executive Committee (www
body:
**Fellow Democrat,**
**Last night’s Democratic victories are victories for all West Virginians. From the picket line to the ballot box, West Virginians are proving now more than ever that they’ve had enough! Enough of being left behind and enough of the attacks on working families by the Republican leadership in this state.[Have you had enough?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/1184827/6563820/-1315516360?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzgyNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjYWM2MDkxNS1lOTUzLWU4MTEtODBjMy0wMDE1NWRhNzgwMTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=indGGGSGDkOZwivRYp5-EHZQq_UrVuv38_F_F24jL44=)**
**Teachers proved earlier this year, that even though Republicans shut doors in our faces, put us at the bottom while they prioritize the top 1%, and continued their attacks on our wages, jobs, and benefits we will KEEP GOING.**
